steps.title: Set up the root-finding method
steps.content:
This is a quadratic equation, so the goal is to find its roots. The quadratic formula is a direct way to solve it when simple factoring is not the focus. For $4x^2 + 5x - 5 = 0$ with $a = 4$, $b = 5$, $c = -5$:
$$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} = \dfrac{-5 \pm \sqrt{25 - 4(4)(-5)}}{8} = \dfrac{-5 \pm \sqrt{105}}{8}$$

steps.title: Pick the greater root
steps.content:
The greatest solution uses the $+$ sign:
$$x = -\dfrac{5}{8} + \dfrac{\sqrt{105}}{8}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
